z.GDB commands for working with pretty-printers.
Internal utility to parse a pretty-printer command argv.
    Arguments:
        arg: The arguments to the command.  The format is:
             [object-regexp [name-regexp]].
             Individual printers in a collection are named as
             printer-name;subprinter-name.
    Returns:
        The result is a 3-tuple of compiled regular expressions, except that
        the resulting compiled subprinter regexp is None if not provided.
    Raises:
        SyntaxError: an error processing ARG
    
too many arguments
invalid object regexp: %sz
invalid name regexp: %sz
invalid subname regexp: %s)
gdbZ
string_to_argv
SyntaxError
split
compile)
argvZ
argcZ
object_regexpZ
name_regexpZ
subname_regexpZ
name_subname
	object_re
name_re
subname_re
4/usr/share/gdb/python/gdb/command/pretty_printers.py
parse_printer_regexps
z>Internal utility to see if printer (or subprinter) is enabled.
enabledTN)
hasattrr
printerr
printer_enabled_pL
InfoPrettyPrintera
GDB command to list all registered pretty-printers.
    Usage: info pretty-printer [OBJECT-REGEXP [NAME-REGEXP]]
    OBJECT-REGEXP is a regular expression matching the objects to list.
    Objects are "global", the program space's file, and the objfiles within
    that program space.
    NAME-REGEXP matches the name of the pretty-printer.
    Individual printers in a collection are named as
    printer-name;subprinter-name.c
info pretty-printer)
superr
__init__r
COMMAND_DATA
self
	__class__r
InfoPrettyPrinter.__init__c

 [disabled]N)

z InfoPrettyPrinter.enabled_stringc

z9Return a 2-tuple of number of enabled and total printers.r
totalr
count_enabled_printers
zkReturn a 2-tuble of the enabled state and total number of all printers.
    This includes subprinters.
    r
enabled_count

zZPrint the number of printers enabled/disabled.
    We count subprinters individually.
    z
%d of %d printers enabledN)
#show_pretty_printer_enabled_summary
Worker for enabling/disabling pretty-printers.
    Arguments:
        pretty_printers: list of pretty-printers
        name_re: regular-expression object to select printers
        subname_re: regular expression object to select subprinters or None
                    if all are affected
        flag: True for Enable, False for Disable
    Returns:
        The number of printers affected.
        This is just for informational purposes for the user.
    r
flagrA
do_enable_pretty_printer_1
)	z7Internal worker for enabling/disabling pretty-printers.r
disabledz
%d %s %sr
stater
do_enable_pretty_printer-
EnablePrettyPrintera
GDB command to enable the specified pretty-printer.
    Usage: enable pretty-printer [OBJECT-REGEXP [NAME-REGEXP]]
    OBJECT-REGEXP is a regular expression matching the objects to examine.
    Objects are "global", the program space's file, and the objfiles within
    that program space.
    NAME-REGEXP matches the name of the pretty-printer.
    Individual printers in a collection are named as
    printer-name;subprinter-name.c
enable pretty-printer)
EnablePrettyPrinter.__init__c
EnablePrettyPrinter.invoke
DisablePrettyPrintera
GDB command to disable the specified pretty-printer.
    Usage: disable pretty-printer [OBJECT-REGEXP [NAME-REGEXP]]
    OBJECT-REGEXP is a regular expression matching the objects to examine.
    Objects are "global", the program space's file, and the objfiles within
    that program space.
    NAME-REGEXP matches the name of the pretty-printer.
    Individual printers in a collection are named as
    printer-name;subprinter-name.c
disable pretty-printer)
DisablePrettyPrinter.__init__c
FNrQ
DisablePrettyPrinter.invokerS
zDCall from a top level script to install the pretty-printer commands.N)
 register_pretty_printer_commands
Commandr
<module>
